readme: Generated from src/_data/blocks/*.json by scripts/gen-cem.mjs (WE block protocols → CEM). Do not edit by hand.

declarations.name: ActionButtonBehavior
declarations.description: Behavioral implementation of the Action Intent, handling visual priority rendering, busy state layout (width locking, visibility), and platform-aware group ordering.

traits.name: withBusyDisabled
traits.description: Disables the action during pending state (aria-disabled + pointer-events:none)

traits.name: withBusyInteractive
traits.description: Keeps the action interactive during pending state (reduced opacity indicator)

traits.name: withPlatformOrdering
traits.description: Reorders action groups based on OS conventions (macOS: primary last, Windows: primary first)

traits.name: withWidthLock
traits.description: Captures computed width before busy state, applies as inline min-width to prevent layout shift

invokerCommands.pattern: commandfor/command attributes for declarative action invocation
invokerCommands.adoption: Action buttons can use commandfor to target Resource Action elements; command attribute specifies the mutation

ariaDisabled.pattern: aria-disabled keeps element focusable but non-interactive
ariaDisabled.adoption: withBusyDisabled applies aria-disabled instead of HTML disabled, preserving tab order and screen reader announcements

ariaLive.pattern: aria-live regions for dynamic content announcements
ariaLive.adoption: Busy state changes announced via aria-live='polite' on the button's label region

cssContainment.pattern: CSS contain: layout for isolation from reflow
cssContainment.adoption: Action groups use contain: layout to prevent busy-state width changes from triggering ancestor reflow

declarations.name: createAppShell
declarations.description: Chrome layout primitive that frames a page: a (sticky) header with a brand lockup and a header-controls region, an optional nav landmark, the main content region (preceded by a skip link), and an optional footer. Built for the #870 must-build set so the #865 WE-docs chrome dogfood can mount it in-document (mode C).

landmarkRoles.pattern: header / nav / main / footer landmark elements structure the page for assistive technology
landmarkRoles.adoption: App Shell emits native <header>, <nav aria-label>, <main>, <footer> landmark regions

skipLink.pattern: A skip link as the first focusable element lets keyboard users bypass repeated chrome
skipLink.adoption: App Shell renders a skip link targeting the focusable <main> region

positionSticky.pattern: position: sticky pins the header to the top on scroll without JavaScript
positionSticky.adoption: Sticky header is the default; opt out via config

declarations.name: renderAuditTimeline
declarations.description: Reference render for the Audit Timeline intent, the visual member of the Web Audit protocol. `renderAuditTimeline` renders an entity's `AuditEvent[]` as a chronological, filterable feed across the intent's `density` (compact|comfortable), `grouping` (flat|by-day|by-actor), and `detail` (summary|expanded) dimensions, with `role="list"` and `<time datetime>` semantics. It owns the display, not the record — append-only semantics, actor attribution, and before/after belong to the protocol. Ships an element form and a string form (`auditTimelineHTML`).

headingElements.usage: Renders an entity’s `AuditEvent[]` as an ordered `<ol>` timeline; each event’s moment uses `<time datetime>` for a machine-readable, accessibly-announced timestamp.

declarations.name: DefaultAuditProvider
declarations.description: Reference runtime for the Web Audit protocol (webaudit / #357) — a domain entity's immutable, actor-attributed history. Ships the protocol's seam: the normalized `AuditEvent` ({ target, action, actor, at, before?, after?, correlationId? }, before/after reusing the Web States `ChangeRecord` shape), the swappable `CustomAuditProvider` contract (`append()` / `queryByEntity()` / optional `subscribe()`), a native-first append-only `DefaultAuditProvider` (it freezes each event — never mutates or deletes), and the `CustomAuditRegistry` (`window.customAudit`). The `auditLifecycle()` helper is the headline composition: it subscribes a Web Lifecycle provider so every transition becomes one `AuditEvent` automatically. First consumer: the loan-origination exercise app (#317).

headingElements.usage: Reference runtime for the Web Audit protocol: an immutable, actor-attributed `AuditEvent[]` history; each entry timestamps with the `<time>` element’s machine-readable `datetime`.

declarations.name: Autocomplete
declarations.description: The editable, filterable concrete member of the droplist family — an <input role="combobox"> bound to a separate role="listbox" surface (<auto-complete>). The richest member: typing narrows the collection (filter) instead of seeking within it (type-ahead), and DOM focus stays on the input while virtual focus moves through the listbox. A form-associated custom element so <form>/FormData/reset/validity work like a native control, mirroring the WAI-ARIA APG Combobox (list-autocomplete) pattern.

ariaCombobox.usage: An `<input role="combobox">` with `aria-autocomplete="list"`, `aria-expanded`, and `aria-controls` bound to a separate `role="listbox"`; the active option is tracked with `aria-activedescendant` as the input filters.

declarations.name: BackgroundTaskSurface
declarations.description: The persistent app-shell surface that hosts long-running work after it goes async — the receiving end of a Loader's escalation:async handoff. Renders one entry per registered task with per-task progress (the Loader's own state machine, hosted off-view), per-task retry delegating to Reliability, and arms the navigation-away / before-unload guard while any task is in flight. Distinct from the Feedback Intent (transient toasts) and the Loader Intent (in-view pending state that dies with its view).

events.name: background-task-register
events.description: The handoff IN. A Loader (or any producer) dispatches this when work goes async; the nearest ancestor surface adopts the carried task handle and begins rendering its entry. This is the block's registration mechanism.

events.name: background-task-state-change
events.description: Re-emitted by the surface as a task entry moves through the intent lifecycle. Surfaces the Background Task Intent's registered → active → success → error → dismissed states as a single observable event.

events.name: background-task-retry
events.description: Fired when the user activates retry on a failed entry. The surface only exposes the affordance; recovery policy is delegated to the Reliability Intent. Cancelable so a host can intercept and run its own recovery.

events.name: background-task-dismiss
events.description: Fired when an entry is removed — by the user, or by auto-clear for a transient entry that resolved successfully. Cancelable to allow a host to keep a sticky entry.

traits.name: withBatchAggregation
traits.description: Present multiple concurrent tasks, each with its own progress and retry, where one task's failure does not abort the others (default is single — one entry at a time)

traits.name: withStickyEntries
traits.description: Keep a resolved entry until explicitly dismissed instead of auto-clearing on success (error entries are sticky regardless, so a failure that happened off-view is still reviewable)

traits.name: withNavigationGuard
traits.description: While any task is active, arm the beforeunload prompt (cross-document close/reload) and the Navigation API route-leave confirm (same-document); cleared when the last in-flight task resolves

traits.name: withCompletionToast
traits.description: Emit a transient Feedback Intent toast when a task completes, for the case where the user is on another view and the rail entry auto-clears

traits.name: withPerTaskRetry
traits.description: Expose a retry affordance on a failed entry that delegates recovery to the Reliability Intent (the surface owns the affordance, not the policy)

traits.name: withReloadDurability
traits.description: Opt into reload-durable work via the Background Fetch / service-worker adapter so an in-flight task survives a full page reload or tab close (default is route-only, in-memory)

customEvent.pattern: Bubbling CustomEvent as the registration handoff
customEvent.adoption: A Loader at escalation:async dispatches a bubbling background-task-register event carrying its loader-state handle; the surface listens and adopts it. A one-shot, fire-and-handoff registration that decouples the surface from the (possibly torn-down) originating element — chosen over an injector/DI registry because the task must outlive its originating view.

beforeunload.pattern: beforeunload prompt for cross-document leave / close
beforeunload.adoption: While navigationGuard:warn and any task is active, the surface arms beforeunload so closing the tab or a full reload prompts the user. Cleared automatically when the last in-flight task resolves. This guard is why the route-only baseline is safe: a hard reload that would lose in-flight work is gated, not silently dropped.

navigationApi.pattern: Navigation API navigate event intercept for same-document route-leave
navigationApi.adoption: The SPA route-leave confirm half of navigationGuard:warn intercepts the Navigation API's navigate event (preventing/confirming same-document navigations) — the same-document counterpart to beforeunload. The two primitives present as one guard contract (harvested as the navigation-guard intent, #129).

backgroundFetch.pattern: Background Fetch API + service worker for reload-durable work
backgroundFetch.adoption: The durability:reload tier — work that genuinely survives a full reload or tab close — is delegated here, NOT baked into the baseline. The intent borrows Background Fetch's vocabulary 'without requiring a service worker', so this surface stays usable as a route-only in-memory baseline and treats reload-durability as an opt-in adapter (see backlog spin-off).

ariaStatusRegion.pattern: Live status region for off-view progress announcement
ariaStatusRegion.adoption: Because the surface is off the user's current view, task completion and failure are announced via a polite live region (role=status) so a screen-reader user learns a backgrounded task finished without watching the rail. Reuses the Live Region Status paradigm rather than redefining it.

declarations.name: Breadcrumb
declarations.description: A structured trail showing the user's location within a site or data hierarchy, as an ordered list of links from root to current. Native-first: it IS a <nav aria-label="Breadcrumb"> wrapping an ordered list of <a> elements, with the current page a plain non-link node carrying aria-current="page" — the block adds only what the bare markup cannot express alone: overflow collapse when the trail is too wide for its container, and a separator that is presentational (CSS ::before / aria-hidden), never an announced character. It composes the Navigation Intent (it is a navigation landmark) and the Typography Intent (the responsible-truncation paradigm for a long crumb label). Deliberately distinct from nav-list (APG Disclosure Navigation menus), from tabs (panel switching), and from a stepper (a linear process, not a location).

navigationLandmark.usage: A `<nav aria-label="Breadcrumb">` wrapping an ordered list of links from root to current, the current page marked `aria-current="page"`.

ariaCurrent.usage: The trail’s final, current location carries `aria-current="page"`.

declarations.name: BreakpointObserver
declarations.description: Runtime implementation of the Breakpoint Intent, mapping semantic step names to concrete CSS media queries or container queries and exposing reactive state.

traits.name: withMobileFirst
traits.description: Accumulative min-width queries — start small, add complexity as space grows

traits.name: withDesktopFirst
traits.description: Subtractive max-width queries — start large, remove features as space shrinks

traits.name: withStrictRange
traits.description: Isolated range queries — each tier is an independent bucket with no accumulation

traits.name: withContainerQueries
traits.description: Component-scoped breakpoints using CSS container queries instead of viewport media queries

containerQueries.usage: Maps semantic breakpoint step names to concrete CSS media or container queries and exposes the active step reactively, observing via `matchMedia` / container-query matching.

declarations.name: BroadcastBehavior
declarations.description: Declarative DOM-to-JS bridge for provider-scoped broadcast events. Fills the platform gap between targeted Invoker Commands and global event dispatch.

invokerCommands.gap: No broadcast equivalent — commands are always 1-to-1 via element ID

commandEvent.pattern: CommandEvent is constructable but designed for declarative button triggers, not programmatic broadcast

customEvent.pattern: CustomEvent + dispatchEvent is the current broadcast mechanism
customEvent.gap: Globally-scoped string names — no discrimination, no scoping

declarations.name: createButton
declarations.description: Chrome button family: icon-only buttons, icon links (rendered as <a> when given an href, e.g. the GitHub control), and aria-pressed/aria-expanded toggle buttons (the mobile-nav hamburger). Built for the #870 must-build set so the #865 WE-docs chrome dogfood can mount it in-document (mode C).

ariaPressed.pattern: aria-pressed turns a button into a toggle button with announced on/off state
ariaPressed.adoption: The toggle variant maintains aria-pressed and mirrors aria-expanded when it controls a target

accessibleName.pattern: Icon-only controls need an accessible name via aria-label
accessibleName.adoption: Icon buttons/links set aria-label from the configured label

declarations.name: Carousel
declarations.description: A set of equivalent slides presented one (or a few) at a time, advanced by controls, pagination markers, swipe, or optional autoplay. Native-first by construction: the substrate is a CSS scroll-snap container (scroll-snap-type + scroll-snap-align gives paging, momentum, touch, and keyboard scrolling for free), and where supported the emerging CSS carousel pseudo-elements (::scroll-button() for prev/next, ::scroll-marker / scroll-marker-group for the dots) provide native controls — the block leans on them rather than re-implementing scroll logic in JS. It adds the WAI-ARIA APG carousel accessibility (rotation announced via a live region; autoplay opt-in, paused on hover/focus, honoring prefers-reduced-motion, with a required pause control) and composes the Motion, Live-Region Status, and Navigation intents. Distinct from tabs (labelled panels, not equivalent slides) and from paginated data (the Collection Operations intent).

ariaApgKeyboard.usage: A `role="region"` with `aria-roledescription="carousel"`, slide-picker controls, an accessible label, and a rotation pause control, following the APG carousel pattern.

declarations.name: Checkbox
declarations.description: Native-first over `<input type="checkbox">`: a single boolean control, a checkbox group (multi-select), and the `indeterminate` (mixed) tri-state. Realizes the `input` boolean control and composes `selection` (model: multiple) for groups; the indeterminate state maps to `aria-checked="mixed"` on a group parent. Zero JS for the single/standalone case (the native element IS the block); a thin behavior only coordinates a group's mixed-state parent. Sliced from #468 (form-control inventory).

attributes.name: checked
attributes.description: The boolean checked state of the native `<input type="checkbox">`.

attributes.name: indeterminate
attributes.description: The mixed tri-state — maps to `aria-checked="mixed"` on a group parent.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Disables the control; excluded from form submission and the tab order.

attributes.name: required
attributes.description: Marks the control required for native constraint validation (`:user-invalid`).

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: Form field name the value is submitted under.

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: Value submitted when checked.

members.name: checked
members.description: Gets or sets the checked state.

members.name: indeterminate
members.description: Gets or sets the mixed tri-state (display-only; not submitted).

constraintValidation.usage: Native-first over `<input type="checkbox">`: a single boolean, a multi-select group, and the `indeterminate` (mixed) tri-state, with native required-validity.

declarations.name: CodeViewElement
declarations.description: Renders syntax-highlighted component source with a copy affordance — the source-code-view feature from the workbench inventory (#625), ratified by #626 as a forced block mint. The copy affordance composes the data-transfer copy intent (the copy-out half). Native-first: highlighting paints token ranges via the CSS Custom Highlight API over a tokenizer rather than wrapping every token in a <span>, with library highlighters (Prism/Shiki/highlight.js) as opt-in adapters. A live-editable sandbox embed is explicitly out of scope (that is the assembler/playground concern).

cssCustomHighlight.pattern: CSS Custom Highlight API — paint token ranges via Highlight + ::highlight() without per-token DOM
cssCustomHighlight.adoption: The native-first highlighter tokenizes the source and registers a Highlight per token class, styled with ::highlight(); no <span> soup, and the source text stays a single selectable/copyable node.

clipboard.pattern: Async Clipboard API (navigator.clipboard.writeText) behind the data-transfer copy intent
clipboard.adoption: The copy button writes the verbatim source via the data-transfer copy-out (emit) half, so the affordance is the shared intent, not a bespoke clipboard call.

declarations.name: CollectionOperationsModule
declarations.description: The headless coordinator that owns one collection and runs the WHOLE collection-operations pipeline — filter -> sort -> group -> **page** — over it, then hands the current page slice to the data-table for rendering and the totals to the pagination block. The two stage-owning blocks (data-table: filter/sort/group; pagination: page) each run one stage; until this existed no one ran the pipeline as a whole, so the obvious consumer wiring (page-then-render) sorted only the current page, not the whole book (surfaced by the loan-origination app, #317). This coordinator applies filter/sort/group across the entire set first (reusing the data-table's verified `applyPipeline`, whose `page` stage is a deliberate no-op), then windows the result — order is always collection-wide and the page is just the visible slice. Re-feeding the already-ordered slice back through the data-table is safe (filter/sort/group are idempotent on a processed slice). Ships a standalone `CollectionOperationsBehavior` and an optional headless `<collection-operations>` element that routes descendant `data-table-change` / `pagination-change` events and emits `collection-operations-change` — not a documented composition and not a rendered block (#452 ruling A/A).

intlCollator.usage: The headless filter → sort → group → page coordinator; locale-aware sort comparisons go through `Intl.Collator`.

arrayToSorted.usage: Sorting is non-mutating via `Array.prototype.toSorted`, leaving the source collection untouched.

declarations.name: parseDefinition
declarations.description: The <component> element — defines a brand-new custom element entirely from HTML (tag name, template, shadow options). The fourth gap in Web Components, alongside scoped registries, injector integration, and transient components. Intended implementation: a deterministic build-time AST transform to a class-based element.

customElements.pattern: Autonomous custom elements registered via customElements.define()
customElements.adoption: The <component> definition lowers to an autonomous element class + customElements.define(). Autonomous only (Safari refuses customized built-ins).

declarativeShadowDOM.pattern: <template shadowrootmode> attaches a shadow root during HTML parsing, no JS
declarativeShadowDOM.adoption: shadow="open|closed" maps onto attachShadow / DSD. The component's children become the element's shadow content. attachShadow options forward as boolean attributes — delegates-focus maps onto attachShadow({ delegatesFocus: true }) / shadowrootdelegatesfocus.

htmlTemplate.adoption: The component's own children are the render source (implicit template, DC-11) — cloned into the shadow (or light) tree on connect. A lone direct-child <template> is the explicit, inert form for un-built runtime delivery or parser-sensitive markup; both lower identically.

slot.pattern: <slot> projects light-DOM children into shadow content
slot.adoption: Content projection is native — the definition uses <slot> directly; no custom projection layer.

scopedRegistries.adoption: Tier-2 scope attribute composes with the project's Scoped Registries section; global customElements by default.

declarativeCustomElements.pattern: <definition name constructor> — define a custom element from markup
declarativeCustomElements.adoption: Prior art. We choose <component> over <definition>; we ship the static tier now and compose the unshipped binding primitives (Template Instantiation, DOM Parts) later.

elementInternals.pattern: attachInternals() exposes form participation (static formAssociated), default ARIA semantics (internals.role), and custom states (internals.states / :state())
elementInternals.adoption: form-associated maps onto static formAssociated + attachInternals(); default-role maps onto internals.role default semantics. Custom states and form value/validation wiring are deferred (DC-14, compose with Web Validation).

atomicMove.pattern: Element.moveBefore() relocates a connected node without disconnecting it; a connectedMoveCallback opts custom elements into state preservation
atomicMove.adoption: preserve-on-move emits an empty connectedMoveCallback() so moveBefore() preserves the element's state (focus, media, iframes, shadow) instead of teardown/rebuild.

declarations.name: CompositeWidget
declarations.description: Focus management and navigation protocol for complex widgets like Grids, Trees, and Menus, implementing the Roving Tabindex pattern.

rovingTabindex.usage: Implements the Roving Tabindex focus model for composite widgets (grids, trees, menus): exactly one descendant is `tabindex="0"`, all others `tabindex="-1"`, and the value moves with focus so the widget is a single Tab stop.

declarations.name: DataGridModule
declarations.description: The interactive twin of the Data Table block: same tabular data, the *other* WAI-ARIA APG pattern. Renders a native `<table role="grid">` of `role="row"` / `role="columnheader"` / `role="gridcell"` cells with cell-level keyboard navigation — arrow keys move one cell (clamping at the edges), Home/End jump to the row ends, Ctrl+Home/Ctrl+End to the grid corners, PageUp/PageDown by a page — over a roving tabindex (exactly one cell is `tabindex="0"`, every other is `-1`). It is the concrete consumer of the Focus Delegation intent (strategy `roving`, orientation `both`); the column-header row is itself navigable. Where the Data Table is read-and-reorder (`role="table"` + `aria-sort`), the Data Grid is navigate-the-cells (`role="grid"` + roving focus) — APG documents them as separate patterns, so they are parallel blocks, not one upgraded in place.

traits.name: withCellNavigation
traits.description: Realizes the APG Data Grid keyboard model over a roving tabindex — arrows (clamped), Home/End, Ctrl+Home/End, PageUp/Down — moving the single `tabindex="0"` cell with DOM focus.

traits.name: withGridRoles
traits.description: Projects the tabular data onto the `role="grid"` / `role="row"` / `role="columnheader"` / `role="gridcell"` structure with `aria-rowcount` / `aria-colcount` and per-cell `aria-rowindex` / `aria-colindex`.

apgDataGrid.pattern: A `role="grid"` of `role="row"` rows and `role="gridcell"` / `role="columnheader"` cells with arrow-key cell navigation and a roving tabindex
apgDataGrid.adoption: DataGridBehavior renders the native `<table>` with the grid roles and realizes the APG keyboard model verbatim (arrows clamp, Home/End to row ends, Ctrl+Home/End to grid corners, PageUp/Down by a page); the header row is navigable

rovingTabindex.pattern: Exactly one descendant is `tabindex="0"` (the active item) and all others are `tabindex="-1"`; the value moves with focus
rovingTabindex.adoption: The active cell carries `tabindex="0"`, every other cell `-1`; a keystroke recomputes the active cell and the tabindex roves to it, so Tab enters/leaves the grid as one stop while arrows move within

ariaGridIndices.pattern: `aria-rowcount` / `aria-colcount` on the grid and `aria-rowindex` / `aria-colindex` on rows and cells declare the full extent and a cell's position
ariaGridIndices.adoption: The grid declares `aria-rowcount` / `aria-colcount` (header row included) and each row/cell carries its 1-based `aria-rowindex` / `aria-colindex`, so the position is announced even when not every row is realized in the DOM

declarations.name: DataTableModule
declarations.description: The blocks-layer home for the Collection Operations `filter`, `sort`, and `group` stages (the `page` stage is the companion Pagination block — compose, never merge). Renders a native `<table>` whose sortable column headers project `sortDirection` onto the WAI-ARIA APG `aria-sort` attribute (ascending/descending, sortable-but-unsorted = none) via button-wrapped `<th scope="col">`, orders rows with `Intl.Collator` (sensitivity/numeric/caseFirst/ignorePunctuation) and SQL-style empty placement, narrows them by `filterMatch` (all/any), and partitions them into `<tbody>` groups with `groupSummary` aggregates named after the SQL functions (count/sum/average/minimum/maximum). The pipeline order is data (default filter → sort → group → page). Ships a consumable `DataTableBehavior` (and a `<data-table>` custom element) that mounts the verified renderer, wires click-to-sort + a polite live-region announcement, and emits `data-table-change`; its first enterprise consumer is the loan-origination exercise app (#317).

traits.name: withSortableHeader
traits.description: Projects the active sort key's direction onto `aria-sort` on a button-wrapped `<th scope="col">` (the `sort:header` seam). Sortable-but-unsorted headers are `aria-sort="none"`.

traits.name: withFilter
traits.description: Narrows the rows by combining call-site predicates per `filterMatch` (all = AND, any = OR).

traits.name: withGrouping
traits.description: Partitions rows into `<tbody>` groups, each led by a `scope="rowgroup"` summary row carrying the `groupSummary` aggregate (count/sum/average/minimum/maximum).

apgSortableTable.pattern: Tabular data in a native `<table>` with `<th scope="col">` headers; sortable headers wrap their label in a `<button>` and the active column carries `aria-sort`
apgSortableTable.adoption: DataTableBehavior renders the table and projects the primary sort key onto `aria-sort` on its `<th>`; sortable-but-unsorted headers are `aria-sort="none"`, non-sortable headers carry none

ariaSort.pattern: aria-sort = ascending | descending | none | other, on one header at a time
ariaSort.adoption: The intent's `sortDirection` projects verbatim onto `aria-sort`; only the primary sort key's header is marked (APG: one sorted header at a time), even under a multi-key sort

intlCollator.adoption: The reference comparator is `Intl.Collator`; the intent's text-comparison dimensions adopt its option names verbatim, so `numeric:true` gives natural order (img2 before img10) with no bespoke comparator

arrayToSorted.adoption: Stable sort makes multi-key tiebreaking and `sortKeys:single` (which preserves prior order) well-defined without extra bookkeeping

rowgroupScope.pattern: A `<th scope="rowgroup">` associates a header with all cells in its row group
rowgroupScope.adoption: Each group renders as its own `<tbody>` led by a `scope="rowgroup"` summary row carrying the group key + its aggregate, so assistive tech announces the grouping

declarations.name: DataTransferZoneElement
declarations.description: A drop-zone runtime that collapses the three native sources of a DataTransfer payload — drag-and-drop, clipboard paste, and <input type=file> — into one normalized `receive` event under a declared `accepts` contract (type/size), dispatching `reject` for anything that fails, with a keyboard/a11y file-picker affordance, the copy-vs-move flow as the drag dropEffect, and a copy-out (`emit`) half.

events.name: receive
events.description: An accepted, normalized payload entered the zone (from drop, paste, or the file input)

events.name: reject
events.description: An incoming payload failed the declared accepts contract (kind, type, or size)

events.name: emit
events.description: The zone wrote a typed payload onto an outgoing drag or copy (the emit flow)

dataTransfer.pattern: The native DataTransfer object shared by drag events and the clipboard
dataTransfer.adoption: normalizeDataTransfer() reads DataTransferItemList (file vs string kinds) with a files+getData fallback, so drop and paste collapse to one shape.

dragAndDrop.pattern: HTML Drag and Drop API (dragover.preventDefault to become a drop target; dropEffect)
dragAndDrop.adoption: The zone mirrors its configured copy/move effect onto dataTransfer.dropEffect during dragover.

clipboardEvent.adoption: Paste funnels through the same normalizer; copy writes the queued emit payload via setData.

fileInput.pattern: <input type=file multiple> as the keyboard-accessible source
fileInput.adoption: An always-present hidden file input behind a real <button> gives a keyboard/a11y path; drag and paste are pointer/clipboard sugar on top.

declarations.name: DatePicker
declarations.description: The first named shallow preset over the abstract `temporal` core: a single calendar-date picker. It pins the Temporal Intent to presentation=media (a spatial calendar grid) and granularity=point (one date), bound to the native <input type="date"> anchor. It adds nothing the core lacks — it is the core with two dimensions pinned and one anchor chosen — so it inherits locale-aware parse/format, keyboard roving, and the native form-control baseline for free. Native <input type="date"> stays the zero-JS fallback; the calendar-grid surface is the media-presentation enhancement.

ariaApgKeyboard.usage: A single calendar-date preset: native `<input type="date">` or a grid calendar dialog with arrow-key day navigation.

declarations.name: DateRangePicker
declarations.description: A named shallow preset over the abstract `temporal` core: a from–to date range. It pins the Temporal Intent to presentation=media (a calendar grid) and granularity=range (two coordinated, ordered dates), bound to two native <input type="date"> anchors (start, end). The range coordination — the start date can't cross the end date, reported as a pair — is the core's machinery, exactly as the slider's dual-thumb range is two bound, ordered values over one track; this preset only pins granularity=range and binds the two anchors.

ariaApgKeyboard.usage: A from–to range preset over the calendar grid: two coupled date values with range-selection keyboard handling.

declarations.name: DatetimePicker
declarations.description: A named shallow preset over the abstract `temporal` core: a combined date-and-time picker. It pins the Temporal Intent to presentation=media and granularity=point, bound to the native <input type="datetime-local"> anchor — one control that selects both a calendar date and a time. It composes the core's calendar + clock surfaces and inherits locale-aware parse/format and keyboard roving; the native datetime-local input is the zero-JS fallback.

ariaApgKeyboard.usage: A combined date-and-time preset: native `<input type="datetime-local">` or a composed calendar + clock surface.

declarations.name: renderDecisionTrace
declarations.description: Reference render for the Decision Trace intent, the visual member of the Web Decisions protocol (decision-record / #355). `renderDecisionTrace(record)` turns a normalized `DecisionRecord` — why a rules/criteria evaluation reached its outcome — into a criteria trace table (input/operator/threshold/outcome per criterion) plus the aggregated outcome and reason codes, across the intent's `layout` (table|list), `emphasis` (all-criteria|deciding), and `disclosure` dimensions. Schema-only standard: the render owns the explanation UI, not the rules. Composes the status-indicator block for the outcome and per-criterion tokens. Ships an element form and a string form (`decisionTraceHTML`).

headingElements.usage: Renders a decision record as a structured, heading-delimited trace (context → options → ruling), using semantic headings and lists for screen-reader navigability.

declarations.name: Dialog
declarations.description: The abstract substrate every dialog-based surface conforms to — a top-layer surface that interrupts or overlays the page, owning the shared modal-surface machinery: focus containment, escape/outside/backdrop dismissal, scroll-lock, modal-vs-non-modal mode, and the native-first top-layer mount. Like droplist, it is not a shippable widget — it is the family substrate. Its concrete members are each their own block, distinct configurations of the Modal Intent's placement over this one substrate: a centered modal (placement=center), an edge Drawer (placement=start/end + slide, #360), and a bottom Sheet (placement=bottom). Native-first by construction: built on the native <dialog> element (showModal for the modal focus-trap, inert background, and ::backdrop; the platform top layer) and the Popover API for the non-modal case — never a position:fixed + z-index re-implementation. It realizes the Modal Intent and composes Focus Containment (the trap), Surface (elevation/material), and Motion (enter/exit).

attributes.name: open
attributes.description: Whether the surface is shown; reflects the native `<dialog>` open state (top-layer mount).

attributes.name: modal
attributes.description: Modal (`showModal()` — focus trap + inert background + `::backdrop`) vs non-modal (Popover API) mounting.

members.name: open
members.description: Gets or sets the shown state; opening moves focus into the surface, closing restores it to the trigger.

members.name: returnValue
members.description: The value set when the dialog closes (native `<dialog>` returnValue), e.g. which button dismissed it.

cssParts.name: panel
cssParts.description: The surface container that holds the content.

dialog.usage: The shared modal-surface substrate over the native `<dialog>` element: top-layer rendering, a backdrop, focus trapping, and restore-focus-on-close.

ariaApgKeyboard.usage: Realizes the APG modal-dialog keyboard model: focus moves into the dialog on open, Tab cycles within it, Escape closes, and focus returns to the trigger.

declarations.name: DoubleCurlyBracketParser
declarations.description: Text node parser for Mustache-style {{expression}} syntax. Detects {{ and }} delimiters in text content.

domParts.usage: Detects Mustache-style `{{expression}}` delimiters in text content and emits binding holes for the interpolation pipeline (the template-parts contract).

declarations.name: DoubleSquareBracketParser
declarations.description: Text node parser for Polymer-style [[expression]] syntax. Detects [[ and ]] delimiters in text content.

domParts.usage: Detects Polymer-style `[[expression]]` delimiters in text content and emits binding holes for the interpolation pipeline (the template-parts contract).

declarations.name: DraftPersistence
declarations.description: Durable save-and-resume of an entity's working state (the #011 storage facet of webstates): autosave-on-change snapshots restored to the exact field state across sessions, behind a pluggable CustomStorageStrategy (IndexedDB native-first, localStorage degradation, in-memory test fallback), plus the last-writer-wins co-edit primitive (per-editor token + savedAt compare + an "also editing" presence signal over BroadcastChannel/storage).

events.name: coedit-presence
events.description: A peer editor on the same entity was seen (joined/left) — the "X also editing" signal

events.name: coedit-conflict
events.description: A peer saved the same entity — compare savedAt and reconcile by last-writer-wins

indexedDB.pattern: IndexedDB as the durable structured-record store
indexedDB.adoption: IndexedDbStorageStrategy is the native-first default behind the CustomStorageStrategy seam (#011 Fork 1A).

storagePersist.adoption: requestPersistent() best-effort marks the drafts durable so the OS will not evict them under pressure.

broadcastChannel.adoption: CoEditCoordinator broadcasts hello/saved/bye; falls back to localStorage + the storage event where BroadcastChannel is absent.

webStorage.adoption: LocalStorageStrategy is the degradation path when IndexedDB is unavailable; prefixed keys keep it namespaced.

declarations.name: Drawer
declarations.description: The edge-anchored, sliding concrete member of the dialog family — a panel that enters from a viewport edge (a navigation drawer, a filter panel, a detail pane). It does NOT re-implement the modal-surface machinery: it extends the abstract dialog block (focus containment, escape/outside/backdrop dismissal, scroll-lock, modal/non-modal mode, top-layer mount are all inherited) and fixes the configuration to an edge — the Modal Intent's placement=start|end (RTL-aware), with a slide-in/out motion and swipe-to-dismiss added on top of the inherited dismissal. Relationship to the dialog family mirrors dropdown to droplist: a concrete member, not a standalone widget and not a flag on one widget. Its siblings are the centered modal (placement=center) and the bottom sheet (placement=bottom).

attributes.name: open
attributes.description: Whether the drawer is shown; inherits the dialog family top-layer + focus model.

attributes.name: placement
attributes.description: Which viewport edge the panel slides in from (RTL-aware) — the Modal Intent `placement=start|end`.

attributes.name: dismissible
attributes.description: Whether backdrop/outside-click and swipe-to-dismiss close the drawer (on top of the inherited Escape dismissal).

members.name: open
members.description: Gets or sets the shown state; while open as a modal the rest of the page is `inert`.

dialog.usage: The edge-anchored member of the dialog family: the same native `<dialog>` top-layer + focus model, sliding in from a viewport edge.

inertAttribute.usage: While open as a modal, the rest of the page is made `inert` so focus and the accessibility tree are confined to the drawer.

declarations.name: Dropdown
declarations.description: The single-select concrete member of the droplist family — a button-triggered, non-filtering listbox with type-ahead. A form-associated custom element (<drop-list>) so <form>/FormData/reset/validity work like the native <select> baseline, with no hidden input shim.

attributes.name: options
attributes.description: Data source for the listbox — an injector reference (e.g. `options="@countries"`) resolved through the ancestor injector chain, or an inline JSON literal.

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: The selected option's value, reflected to the form-associated element so `<form>`/FormData/reset see it.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: Form field name the selected value is submitted under (native form-association, no hidden input shim).

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Disables the control; excluded from form submission and the tab order.

attributes.name: required
attributes.description: Marks the control required for `:user-invalid` / constraint validation.

members.name: value
members.description: Gets or sets the current selection; setting updates the form value and re-emits the public `change` event.

members.name: options
members.description: The resolved option set, re-exposed on the component-local `@options` injector context for descendants.

events.name: change
events.description: Fires when the user commits a new selection; `detail` carries the selected value. Re-emitted at the `<drop-list>` boundary after the internal `selection-change` is stopped.

cssParts.name: trigger
cssParts.description: The button that opens the listbox and shows the current selection.

ariaListbox.usage: A button-triggered `role="listbox"` of `role="option"`; the trigger owns `aria-expanded` / `aria-controls`, the active option `aria-selected`, with one selected value reflected to the form-associated element.

htmlSelectTypeAhead.usage: Printable-key type-ahead jumps to the next option whose label starts with the typed buffer, matching native `<select>` behaviour.

declarations.name: Droplist
declarations.description: Composition manifest for the droplist family — the abstract substrate every list-based selection surface conforms to (Dropdown, Multi-dropdown, Autocomplete, Tree-select, native <select>). Composes existing intents rather than re-specifying their behavior, and documents the two-channel resolution rule (ambient intent vs. trait selection) that concrete members resolve through.

ariaListbox.usage: The abstract substrate every list-selection member conforms to: a `role="listbox"` of `role="option"` items with single/multi selection and managed focus, realized concretely by Dropdown / Multi-dropdown / Autocomplete / Tree-select.

declarations.name: createOnEventAttribute
declarations.description: Declarative event-to-action mapping using expression syntax with context resolution.

customEvent.usage: Declarative event-to-action mapping: maps DOM/`CustomEvent`s to actions with expression-resolved context, over the standard event model.

declarations.name: FlexRow
declarations.description: Intrinsic auto-flow container that flows and wraps its children by the space actually available to it, realizing the Breakpoint Intent at scope:container. Native-first: the default is CSS @container queries / flex-wrap; ResizeObserver is named as the implementation substrate for environments without container-query support (impl-is-not-a-standard, like CSS Anchor Positioning under webpositioning). The standard-worthy surface is the semantic vocabulary + container-query wiring + the ResizeObserver fallback contract — not the flex CSS itself.

containerQueries.usage: `container-type: inline-size` + `@container` size queries provide the native, script-free reflow path; the FlexRow steps map onto container-query thresholds.

flexWrap.usage: `flex-wrap: wrap` provides the intrinsic auto-flow of children into as many rows as the available inline space allows.

resizeObserver.usage: The non-container-query fallback substrate — observes the container box and re-evaluates the step thresholds on resize, emitting the same reflow contract.

declarations.name: ForEachBehavior
declarations.description: Structural directive for rendering lists of items based on an array.

htmlTemplate.usage: A structural directive that repeats a `<template>` per array item, cloning its content fragment for each row.

declarations.name: FormState
declarations.description: The form-level state machine and lifecycle actions, sitting ABOVE the field-level Validation Intent / Web Validation (which own per-field rules and validity). Web Validation answers 'is this field valid?'; the Form block answers 'what is the state of the whole form, and how do I reset / submit / initialize it?' Grounded in the native `<form>` element, `FormData`, the Constraint Validation API and `formAssociated` custom elements — it adds only the aggregate state and lifecycle actions the platform doesn't expose, and composes existing intents for everything else. Candidate standard (no implementation yet); composition manifest like Droplist.

declarations.name: HandlerExpressionParser
declarations.description: Parser for function-call style event handler expressions. Extends CustomAttributeParser for registry integration.

customEvent.usage: Parses function-call-style event-handler expressions and binds them to the element via `addEventListener`, dispatching/handling `CustomEvent`s through the registry.

declarations.name: InlineTrigger
declarations.description: The runnable trigger-detection engine ratified in #591 (Fork 1-C): an editable region that detects a boundary-anchored trigger char (@/:/#//), slices the query, anchors a popover to the caret (virtual element via the anchor intent), and routes results through the picker surface / autocomplete listbox. Detection-only per Fork 2-A — emits the resolved selection {key, query, range, item} and lets the consumer commit, owning no document model, so it works uniformly in <input>/<textarea>/contenteditable and composes the rich-text standard (#590) for token insertion without being blocked on it. Ships reference text + token insertion adapters but owns neither commit. Forced invariants: APG editable-combobox virtual focus (DOM focus stays in the text, aria-activedescendant drives the listbox), IME-composition suspension, not-a-protocol.

ariaCombobox.usage: A `contenteditable` region that detects a boundary-anchored trigger char (@ / : / # / /) and opens a combobox-style suggestion list bound via `aria-controls` / `aria-activedescendant`.

contentEditable.usage: Trigger detection slices the editable text around the caret using the `contenteditable` selection/range model.

declarations.name: InputFamily
declarations.description: Native-first over the text-ish `<input>` family (text / number / currency / phone / masked): the formatting, masking, and typing affordances the bare element can't express, while keeping the native input as the substrate. Realizes the `input` intent. The platform `type` (`text`/`number`/`tel`/`email`/`url`) carries semantics, keyboard, and validation for free; the block adds a display-format layer (Intl) and an optional input mask over a raw value, never replacing the element. Zero JS for a plain typed input; the format/mask behavior is opt-in. Sliced from #468 (form-control inventory).

constraintValidation.usage: Native-first over the text-ish `<input>` family (text / number / currency / phone / masked): preserves native constraint validation, `inputmode`, and the validity UI the bare element already provides.

declarations.name: InterpolationTextNode
declarations.description: Reactive text node that evaluates expressions and renders their values. Connects text node parsing to the expression parser pipeline.

domParts.usage: A reactive Text node that evaluates an expression and writes its value to `textContent`, the runtime twin of the template-parts / DOM Parts binding model.

declarations.name: KeyboardShortcuts
declarations.description: Standardized keyboard interaction management enabling consistent shortcut handling across shadow boundaries and OS-specific layouts.

keyboardEventKey.usage: Normalizes shortcut handling on `KeyboardEvent.key` / `.code` across shadow boundaries and OS layouts, so chords resolve consistently regardless of where focus sits.

declarations.name: DefaultLifecycleProvider
declarations.description: Reference runtime for the Web Lifecycle protocol (weblifecycle / #353) — a domain entity's declared status set + guarded, role-scoped transition map. Ships the protocol's seam: a data-defined `LifecycleDefinition` (the only lock; portable across engines), the swappable `CustomLifecycleProvider` contract (`available()` + `transition()`), a native-first `DefaultLifecycleProvider` in-memory reference, and the `CustomLifecycleRegistry` (`window.customLifecycles`). Authorization is delegated to a `GuardResolver` (the Web Guards seam; the default is permissive, a real authz provider is async + server-authoritative) and persistence stays the entity's own concern — so the block owns only the state machine. Every applied transition emits a `LifecycleEvent` ({ entity, from, to, actor, at }), the composition seam for audit-trail (#357). First consumer: the loan-origination exercise app (#317).

customEvent.usage: Reference runtime for the Web Lifecycle protocol: a guarded, role-scoped status-transition map; each transition dispatches a `CustomEvent` carrying the from/to status.

declarations.name: MasterDetailBehavior
declarations.description: Reference coordinator for the master-detail intent (#356) — pairs a collection (master list) with a coupled detail region. `MasterDetailBehavior(masterEl, { itemSelector, detailEl, keyOf, renderDetail, focusFlow?, emptyState? })` COMPOSES the shipping selection block (it does not reimplement selection): on select it renders the detail for the item's key (async-aware, toggling an `md-loading` Loader seam), wires the detail as a labelled `role="region"`, applies the focus flow (retain|advance), and handles the empty state (placeholder|collapse), emitting `detail-change`. URL deep-linking is left to the consumer's router (compose, don't duplicate). First consumer: the loan-origination exercise app (#317).

ariaApgKeyboard.usage: Pairs a master list with a coupled detail region: the active list item is `aria-current`, the detail region is `aria-labelledby` the selection and updated as a `aria-controls` target.

declarations.name: Menu
declarations.description: Composition manifest for the action-invocation menu family — the ARIA menu / menubar / menuitem surface for invoking COMMANDS, not selecting values. One block with a shape: menubar | menu | context dimension (orientation derived); roving focus as a fixed mechanic (overriding the droplist family virtual default); every item composes the command intent for invocation; checkable items reuse Selection under a checkable trait; submenus compose Disclosure + Anchor (not the tree-select hierarchy intent); separator and group are owned directly. Distinct from the droplist family, which are listbox/combobox value selectors. Ratified in #173.

ariaApgKeyboard.usage: The action-invocation substrate: `role="menu"` / `role="menubar"` of `role="menuitem"` with the APG keyboard model (arrows move, Enter/Space invoke, Escape closes) — for invoking commands, not selecting values.

declarations.name: Multi-selectDropdown
declarations.description: The multiple-select concrete member of the droplist family — the same button-triggered listbox substrate as the dropdown with one trait flipped: selection.model=multiple, selectionFollowsFocus=false (aria-multiselectable; Space toggles the focused option, arrows move focus only). A form-associated custom element with a multi-value surface (.values array, FormData repeating the name) like the native <select multiple> baseline.

ariaListbox.usage: The dropdown substrate with `aria-multiselectable="true"`: Space toggles each `role="option"`’s `aria-selected`, the trigger summarises the selected set.

declarations.name: NavListBehavior
declarations.description: Accessible navigation menus using the W3C APG Disclosure Navigation pattern. Provides keyboard navigation, collapsible sections, and router integration with aria-current tracking.

disclosureNavigation.pattern: Disclosure Navigation Menu pattern for site navigation
disclosureNavigation.adoption: NavSectionBehavior implements disclosure with aria-expanded and aria-controls

ariaCurrent.pattern: aria-current='page' to indicate the current page within navigation
ariaCurrent.adoption: RouteLinkBehavior sets aria-current='page' on active links, NavListBehavior tracks it for roving tabindex

navigationLandmark.pattern: nav element with aria-label when multiple nav landmarks exist
navigationLandmark.adoption: nav:list attaches to nav elements, documentation recommends aria-label

rovingTabindex.pattern: Focus management using tabindex 0/-1 for keyboard-navigable widgets
rovingTabindex.adoption: NavListBehavior manages roving tabindex across navigation items

prefersReducedMotion.adoption: Demo CSS disables expand/collapse animations when reduced motion preferred

declarations.name: Notification
declarations.description: The runnable surface that realizes the Feedback Intent — a transient, non-blocking message that appears, is accessibly announced, persists for a bounded time (or until dismissed), and is removed. It owns the general notification contract: a top-layer region host plus an injectable controller that app code calls imperatively to enqueue messages, with queueing/stacking, severity-mapped announcement, auto-timeout (paused on hover/focus, forced indefinite when an action is present), and manual dismissal. Presentation styles (toast, snackbar, banner-position, …) are the implementation's choice, not part of the contract — the block defines behaviour, the implementation picks the look. Native-first: the region mounts to the top layer via the Popover API where supported, falling back to a Web Portals mount. Composes the Message Intent (shared severity/tone vocabulary), the Live-Region Status Intent (announcement transport), the Surface Intent (card elevation/material), and the Motion Intent (enter/exit). Deliberately distinct from the OS-level Web Notifications API (system notifications, permission-gated — Web Permissions #009), from background-task-surface (#135, a persistent long-running-task surface), and from inline Message banners (structural, in-flow).

ariaLive.usage: A transient, non-blocking message announced through an `aria-live` region (`polite`/`assertive` by urgency) so it reaches assistive tech without stealing focus.

declarations.name: PaginationModule
declarations.description: The blocks-layer home for the Collection Operations `page` stage. Renders paged controls inside a `<nav aria-label="pagination">` landmark with `aria-current="page"`, syncs the active page to a shareable self-canonical `?page=n` URL via the History API (Navigation API as enhancement; cross-document View Transitions for zero-JS server-rendered transitions), and wires `advance:auto` to an IntersectionObserver scroll sentinel. Numbered / prev-next / load-more presentations over the orthogonal `pageMode` × `advance` axes; infinite scroll = append + auto, never a peer mode. Ships a consumable `PaginationBehavior` (and a `<page-nav>` element) that mounts the verified renderer, delegates page / load-more clicks, clamps + advances the page state, and emits `pagination-change`; first consumer is the loan-origination pipeline (#317).

traits.name: withRangeLabel
traits.description: Surfaces the slice as a 'showing 21-40 of 500' result-range label. Requires a known total -> offset/page protocol only.

traits.name: withPageSizeSelect
traits.description: Adds a page-size selector (e.g. 10 / 25 / 50 / 100). Recommended options and persistence pending #060.

traits.name: withAutoAdvance
traits.description: Turns append into infinite scroll by fetching the next slice at an IntersectionObserver scroll sentinel (advance:auto).

traits.name: withUrlSync
traits.description: Reflects the active page in a shareable, back-button-restorable ?page=n URL via the History API.

paginationNavLandmark.pattern: Pagination controls wrapped in a `<nav aria-label="pagination">` landmark; the active page link marked `aria-current="page"`
paginationNavLandmark.adoption: PaginationBehavior renders the nav landmark and projects the active page as aria-current='page'; each landmark gets a unique label when more than one exists

ariaCurrent.pattern: aria-current='page' marks the active page within the pagination nav
ariaCurrent.adoption: Set on the active page link; the companion sortDirection->aria-sort projection on table headers is deferred to the grid block

historyApi.pattern: pushState/popstate/scrollRestoration sync ?page=n to a shareable, back-button-restorable URL
historyApi.adoption: The native-first baseline for urlSync='query-param'; the active page is a self-canonical ?page=n and restores on back/forward

navigationApi.pattern: A single navigate event intercepts same-origin same-document navigations
navigationApi.adoption: Progressive enhancement over the History API baseline — used when available, never required

crossDocumentViewTransitions.pattern: @view-transition { navigation: auto; } animates full-page navigation between server-rendered ?page=n pages with zero JS
crossDocumentViewTransitions.adoption: The MPA/server-rendered branch opts in via the CSS rule on both pages; no script required

intersectionObserver.pattern: A scroll sentinel observed by IntersectionObserver requests the next slice at the scroll boundary
intersectionObserver.adoption: Wires advance:auto (append + auto = infinite scroll); rendering delegates to Windowed Collection, lifecycle to Loader

selfCanonicalUrls.pattern: Each page is its own unique self-referencing canonical ?page=n; rel=next/prev is deprecated (2019) and never emitted as primary machinery
selfCanonicalUrls.adoption: Server-rendered <a href> page links are the indexable default; load-more/auto content provides a crawlable paginated path alongside the interactive UI

declarations.name: PickerSurface
declarations.description: The one general browse↔search picker surface per #370 Fork 4 (compose, don't reinvent): a `popover` container + the search `input` intent + a 2-D category grid, reusing the droplist/autocomplete substrate rather than a bespoke emoji-picker block. Owns exactly one thing — the load-bearing APG Grid (2-D arrow browse) ↔ Combobox/listbox (search) focus hand-off — and defers everything else to the intent that owns it. One reusable surface instantiated by many specific picker contexts (full-page filtered collection, in-editor tooltip picker, reaction quick-picker), each its own later story.

popoverEvents.usage: One general browse↔search picker built on the Popover API container plus a search `input` and a 2-D category grid — composed, not reinvented.

declarations.name: PrefetchBehavior
declarations.description: Attribute-based behavior that implements the Prefetch Intent by detecting user conditions via browser APIs and triggering resource prefetching.

events.name: prefetch-triggered
events.description: Fired when a prefetch is about to start. Cancel to suppress.

events.name: prefetch-suppressed
events.description: Fired when a prefetch is suppressed due to user conditions (saveData, low battery, slow connection).

events.name: prefetch-complete
events.description: Fired when prefetched data is stored and ready for instant navigation.

traits.name: withViewportPrefetch
traits.description: Triggers prefetch when link enters the viewport via IntersectionObserver

traits.name: withHoverPrefetch
traits.description: Triggers prefetch on pointer hover (>200ms debounce) or focus

traits.name: withInteractionPrefetch
traits.description: Triggers prefetch at pointer down (moment of intent)

speculationRules.adoption: Aligns eagerness model (conservative/moderate/eager/immediate) with our intent levels; uses browser-native prefetch when available

intersectionObserver.pattern: Observes element visibility relative to viewport/ancestor
intersectionObserver.adoption: withViewportPrefetch uses IntersectionObserver with configurable rootMargin to trigger prefetch before element is fully visible

navigatorConnection.adoption: Suppresses prefetch when saveData is true or effectiveType is 'slow-2g'/'2g'

batteryStatus.pattern: Battery Status API: navigator.getBattery() for charging/level
batteryStatus.adoption: Suppresses viewport-level prefetch when battery < 20% and not charging

linkRelPrefetch.adoption: When the prefetched resource is a navigation, injects <link rel="prefetch"> into <head> for browser-native fetching

declarations.name: PropsTableElement
declarations.description: Renders a component's Custom Elements Manifest (#653) as a sortable API reference table — attributes, properties, events, slots, CSS custom-properties and parts. Ratified by #626 Fork 1 (sub-decision): the component API-table renderer consumes the CEM emit rather than a bespoke metadata shape, so one manifest feeds the table, the Technical Configurator args panel, and autodocs. Powers the autodocs capability in the Web Docs catalog surface (#627). Not a new control standard — a derived view over the custom-elements-manifest protocol.

customElementsManifest.pattern: Custom Elements Manifest (custom-elements.json, schema 2.1.0) as the input contract
customElementsManifest.adoption: Reads a CEM declaration (or the whole manifest keyed by tag) and projects its members/attributes/events/slots/cssProperties into table rows — no bespoke metadata shape.

ariaSort.pattern: aria-sort on the sortable column headers (the table is a reference, sortable by name/kind)
ariaSort.adoption: Sorting reuses the collection-operations intent so the column-sort affordance matches every other WE table.

declarations.name: RadioGroup
declarations.description: Native-first over a `<input type="radio">` group sharing a `name`: single-select among mutually-exclusive choices. Realizes the `selection` intent (model: single) and composes `focus-delegation` (roving — arrow keys move AND select within the group, the group is a single Tab stop). Native exclusivity comes free from the shared `name`; a `<fieldset>` + `<legend>` names the group. Zero JS for the standard case. Sliced from #468 (form-control inventory).

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: Shared `name` across the `<input type="radio">` group — native exclusivity comes free from it.

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: The selected option’s value among the mutually-exclusive choices.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Disables the whole group.

attributes.name: required
attributes.description: Requires a selection for native constraint validation.

members.name: value
members.description: Gets or sets the selected value; arrow keys move selection (and focus) within the single Tab stop (roving).

slots.description: The radio options (default slot); a `<fieldset>`/`<legend>` names the group.

rovingTabindex.usage: Native-first over a `<input type="radio">` group sharing a `name`: the group is one Tab stop, arrow keys move selection (and focus) among mutually-exclusive choices.

declarations.name: Reaction
declarations.description: Affix-a-symbol-to-content interaction: each reaction is an `aria-pressed` toggle backed by the Reaction Intent. Composes the expressive-symbol glyph (never re-renders it) and adopts Atlassian ReactionSummary as the contract baseline (count · users[] · reacted · optimisticallyUpdated). The only first-class precedent (Atlassian @atlaskit/reactions) is exactly this block+intent shape. Per #370 Fork 5.

ariaApgKeyboard.usage: Each reaction is an `aria-pressed` toggle button affixing a symbol to content; Space/Enter toggle the pressed state.

declarations.name: reduceReorder
declarations.description: A list whose item order is user-mutable by pointer drag and by keyboard, with live-region announcement and a pluggable commit strategy. Relocates items with atomic Element.moveBefore() so an item keeps its focus/animation/connection state across a move. Manual order — not Collection Operations' computed sort.

events.name: reorder-start
events.description: Fired when an item is grabbed (pointer down on the grab affordance, or keyboard grab key)

events.name: reorder-move
events.description: Fired as the grabbed item passes a new candidate position during a drag

events.name: reorder-commit
events.description: Fired when a move is committed (dropped). Cancelable — preventDefault to reject the new order (e.g. server validation); the commit strategy listens here to persist

events.name: reorder-cancel
events.description: Fired when a grab is abandoned (Esc, or pointer released outside a valid target); order is unchanged

traits.name: withDragHandle
traits.description: Restrict the grab affordance to a scoped handle within each item, leaving the rest interactive

traits.name: withPointerReorder
traits.description: Drag-to-reorder via Pointer Events with pointer capture (mouse, touch, pen)

traits.name: withKeyboardReorder
traits.description: Roving-tabindex keyboard path: grab, move with arrows, drop or cancel

traits.name: withLiveAnnounce
traits.description: Announce each move and the final commit via a polite live region (replaces deprecated aria-grabbed)

traits.name: withCommitStrategy
traits.description: Persist the new order (localStorage or write-back) behind the cancelable reorder-commit event

traits.name: withCrossListReorder
traits.description: Move items between sibling lists that share a group key

pointerEvents.adoption: The default drag path — unifies mouse/touch/pen and captures the pointer for the duration of a drag. Preferred over the HTML5 Drag and Drop API for in-list reorder (better touch + a11y).

elementMoveBefore.adoption: Relocate an item without disconnecting it, preserving focus, animations, iframes, and custom-element connection. Wired declaratively via the Component block's preserve-on-move attribute; falls back to insertBefore where unsupported.

ariaApgDrag.adoption: Roving tabindex over items + grab/move/drop keys; announce moves via a polite live region. The deprecated aria-grabbed/aria-dropeffect are NOT used.

htmlDragAndDrop.pattern: HTML Drag and Drop API (draggable, dragstart/dragover/drop)
htmlDragAndDrop.adoption: Reserved for cross-window and OS file transfer (a data-transfer concern, see backlog #007); NOT the in-list reorder default.

declarations.name: ResourceActionDirective
declarations.description: Manages async mutation lifecycle (POST/PUT/DELETE) — busy state on triggers, double-submit prevention, optimistic updates with automatic rollback on error.

events.name: resource-action-start
events.description: Fired when a mutation begins

events.name: resource-action-end
events.description: Fired when a mutation completes successfully

events.name: resource-action-error
events.description: Fired when a mutation fails

events.name: resource-action-revert
events.description: Fired when an optimistic update is rolled back due to error

traits.name: withBusyTrigger
traits.description: Applies busy state to the triggering element during mutation (width locking, aria-disabled, indicator)

traits.name: withOptimisticMutation
traits.description: Applies predicted result immediately, reverts on error

traits.name: withFeedbackOnComplete
traits.description: Dispatches toast/notification on success or error

fetchAPI.adoption: Action operations delegate to Custom Clients for transport; AbortController for cancellation

invokerCommands.adoption: Action triggers can use Invoker Commands for mutation invocation

ariaDisabled.pattern: aria-disabled for non-focusable disable pattern during pending state
ariaDisabled.adoption: withBusyTrigger applies aria-disabled + pointer-events:none during mutation

declarations.name: ResourceLoader
declarations.description: Trait-based async state management orchestrator handling loading lifecycle with timing, abort, and composable UI traits.

events.name: resource-load-start
events.description: Fired when an async loading operation begins

events.name: resource-load-end
events.description: Fired when an async loading operation completes successfully

events.name: resource-load-error
events.description: Fired when an async loading operation fails

events.name: resource-state-change
events.description: Fired on any state machine transition (idle, pending, loading, success, empty, error, stale)

traits.name: withOptimistic
traits.description: Non-blocking — shows predicted outcome immediately, reverts on failure

traits.name: withSoftBlocking
traits.description: Input-blocking — applies aria-disabled and pointer-events:none while content remains visible

traits.name: withHardBlocking
traits.description: Visual-blocking — obscures or overlays content with a backdrop

traits.name: withReplacement
traits.description: Content-blocking — replaces view with placeholder (skeleton, spinner)

traits.name: withDeterminate
traits.description: Renders measurable progress (0-100%) as a progress bar or percentage

traits.name: withIndeterminate
traits.description: Renders activity indication (spinner, pulse, shimmer) without measurable progress

traits.name: withSkeleton
traits.description: Placeholder content matching page structure for initial loads

traits.name: withStaleWhileRevalidate
traits.description: Keeps previous content visible (dimmed/stale) while refreshing

fetchAPI.pattern: fetch() with AbortController for cancellable requests
fetchAPI.adoption: All loader operations use AbortSignal for cancellation; superseded navigations abort automatically

abortController.adoption: Loaders receive signal param; AbortSignal.timeout() for built-in timeout support

cacheAPI.adoption: Custom Cache sub-standard provides cache policy; runtime can delegate to Cache API

httpCacheControl.adoption: withStaleWhileRevalidate trait mirrors this pattern at the UI level

ariaBusy.pattern: aria-busy for announcing loading state to assistive technology
ariaBusy.adoption: All strategy traits set aria-busy='true' on the loading region during async operations

progressElement.pattern: <progress> element for native determinate progress indication
progressElement.adoption: withDeterminate trait renders native <progress> with value/max attributes

declarations.name: RichTextEditorElement
declarations.description: The rich-text editing Block of the Web Editing standard (#618). Resolves the swappable CustomEditorEngineRegistry (editor-engine protocol, #629) — native-first contenteditable+InputEvent by default, with ProseMirror/Lexical/Slate/Quill registered as alternative engine impls behind the same interface. Implements the rich-text intent and composes the text-formatting intent (#630) for the formatting surface; composes the sanitizer plug (CustomSanitizerRegistry, #631) on paste so external HTML is normalized before it enters the document model. Decorations (active-format ranges, spellcheck/find marks) paint via the CSS Custom Highlight API by default — no <span> soup in the editable region.

contentEditable.pattern: contenteditable + the InputEvent API (beforeinput/input, inputType) as the native-first editing surface
contentEditable.adoption: The default engine drives a contenteditable region through beforeinput, reading inputType to map native editing intents to document-model operations — no library required for the floor.

editContext.pattern: EditContext API — decoupled editing surface for IME/selection without contenteditable quirks
editContext.adoption: Where available, the engine upgrades from the contenteditable floor to EditContext for robust IME composition; the negotiation is the editing-surface capability tier (#628).

cssCustomHighlight.pattern: CSS Custom Highlight API for editor decorations (active format, find, spellcheck ranges)
cssCustomHighlight.adoption: Decorations register as Highlights painted via ::highlight(), so the editable text node stays clean and decorations never corrupt the document model.

declarations.name: RouteViewElement
declarations.description: Declarative client-side routing built on Navigation API, URLPattern, and ViewEngine.

navigationAPI.pattern: Modern API replacing History API with events, traversal info, and interception
navigationAPI.adoption: Primary navigation mechanism, fallback to History API for Safari <17.2

urlPatternAPI.pattern: URLPattern for route pattern matching with named groups
urlPatternAPI.adoption: Use for all route pattern matching, enables :param and * wildcards

viewTransitionsAPI.adoption: Opt-in via route:transition attribute for smooth navigation

speculationRulesAPI.adoption: Generate speculation rules from route definitions for prefetching

historyAPI.adoption: Fallback only for browsers without Navigation API

declarations.name: createSectionedNav
declarations.description: Sectioned-disclosure navigation panel: collapsible section heads (W3C APG Disclosure Navigation) over link lists — the sectioned counterpart of the flat nav-list block. Click toggles a section, Escape collapses the focused one, and the active link carries aria-current. Built for the #870 must-build set so the #865 WE-docs chrome dogfood can mount it in-document (mode C).

disclosureNavigation.pattern: Disclosure Navigation Menu — section heads expose aria-expanded + aria-controls over collapsible link lists
disclosureNavigation.adoption: Each section head is a button with aria-expanded/aria-controls; Escape collapses the focused section

ariaCurrent.pattern: aria-current='page' marks the link for the current page
ariaCurrent.adoption: The current link in a section carries aria-current='page'

declarations.name: SegmentedControl
declarations.description: A single-select choice rendered as a row of adjacent buttons — the iOS/macOS segmented control, or 'pill' toggle group. It is the Selection Intent's single model in a horizontal, always-visible variant (compare radio group / select / switch): every option is visible at once and exactly one is pressed. It composes the Selection Intent (model=single, live immediacy — selecting commits immediately), Focus Delegation (roving tabindex, horizontal orientation with arrow-key movement), and the Typography / Density intents for sizing. Its options are authored as compound children (<segment> elements inside the control) that map to the value model — an instance of the cross-cutting compound-child paradigm (see #176's spin-off) shared with Tabs and the native <select>/<option>.

ariaApgKeyboard.usage: A single-select row of adjacent buttons exposed as a `role="radiogroup"` of `role="radio"` segments — arrow keys move selection, one segment is `aria-checked`.

declarations.name: SelectionBehavior
declarations.description: A runtime for the Collection Operations sibling `selection` intent (model / immediacy / variant / grouping) — the part the draft dropdown/droplist blocks compose but no block shipped. `SelectionBehavior` manages which item(s) in a host container are selected, over items matched by a CSS selector: it projects `aria-selected`, keeps a roving `tabindex`, commits selection live on click and Arrow/Home/End/Enter/Space (single keeps at most one, multiple toggles), and emits a `selection-change` event. The `variant:item` list-selection realization; dropdown/droplist will compose it for listbox selection.

ariaSort.usage: The headless selection runtime (model / immediacy / variant / grouping) backing the droplist members; selected items reflect `aria-selected`, multi-select sets `aria-multiselectable`.

declarations.name: SimpleStore
declarations.description: Concrete implementation of CustomStore with built-in listener management, dot-notation paths, and derived state hooks.

queueMicrotask.usage: A reactive store with dot-notation paths and derived state; listener notifications are batched on the microtask queue (`queueMicrotask`) to coalesce synchronous writes.

declarations.name: Slider
declarations.description: A control for selecting a numeric value — or a min–max range — along a track. It realizes the Selection Intent's range variant (SelectionVariant = 'boolean' | 'item' | 'range'), which no block delivered before. Native-first by construction: a single-thumb slider IS <input type="range">, so the block's job is only the enhancements the native control cannot express alone — a dual-thumb (from–to) range, rich ticks and labelled marks, and locale-aware value formatting (units, number format) surfaced through aria-valuetext. It composes the Selection Intent (variant=range, constraints.min/max), the Input Intent (pointer drag vs keyboard vs touch modality), and the Locale / Translation intents (value formatting). Native stays the baseline; a custom track/thumb is used only when the native element is ruled out.

attributes.name: min
attributes.description: Lower bound of the track (composed Selection Intent `constraints.min`).

attributes.name: max
attributes.description: Upper bound of the track (`constraints.max`).

attributes.name: step
attributes.description: Granularity of value changes along the track.

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: The current value — a single number, or a `[from, to]` pair for a dual-thumb range.

attributes.name: range
attributes.description: Dual-thumb from–to range mode (the Selection range variant native `<input type="range">` cannot express alone).

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Disables the control.

members.name: value
members.description: Gets or sets the value; arrow/Home/End follow the APG slider keyboard model.

members.name: valueText
members.description: Locale-aware formatted value surfaced through `aria-valuetext` (units, number format).

ariaApgKeyboard.usage: A `role="slider"` (single value) or dual-thumb range over a track, arrow/Home/End keyboard model, with `aria-valuemin/now/max`; native `<input type="range">` where applicable.

declarations.name: renderStatusIndicator
declarations.description: Reference render for the Status Indicator intent (#354), the visual member of the Web Lifecycle protocol. `renderStatusIndicator` projects an entity's current lifecycle state onto a semantic status token across the intent's `tone` (neutral|info|progress|positive|caution|critical), `shape` (badge|pill|dot|text), and `affordance` (display-only|actionable) dimensions. It owns the display, not the rules: tone maps to a theme token (never a bespoke hex), the label text carries the state (never colour-only), and `role="status"` marks the standing indicator while a transition announcement composes the Live Region Status intent. Ships an element form and a string form (`statusIndicatorHTML`) for table/innerHTML contexts.

ariaStatusRegion.usage: Projects an entity’s lifecycle status as a labelled badge; status changes are surfaced through a `role="status"` live region.

declarations.name: StepperBehavior
declarations.description: Linear, locked-progression navigation — the Wizard / multi-step-form flow. Realizes the navigation intent's third structure value (`linear`, alongside `lateral`/Tabs and `hierarchical`/Router): a fixed sequence of steps where only the current step is shown, advancing replaces rather than stacks history, and leaving mid-flow can prompt to preserve progress. The block that exercises the navigation intent's `session` persistence value (a step that survives Back/Forward but isn't a fresh shareable link). Reference runtime: `StepperBehavior` (locked progression, `aria-current="step"`, the Step N of M announcement, step-change / step-advance-blocked / flow-complete events); first consumer is the auto-insurance quote wizard (#318).

traits.name: withFreeStepNavigation
traits.description: Relaxes locked progression so any step may be selected at any time (non-gated flows)

traits.name: withResumableState
traits.description: Reflects the current step in the URL so the flow is deep-linkable / resumable

traits.name: withStepValidation
traits.description: Gates advancing on the current step's validity — composes Form validation as the progression gate

traits.name: withOptionalSteps
traits.description: Marks steps that may be skipped without blocking completion

ariaCurrentStep.usage: The current step's indicator carries aria-current="step"; the step list is an ordered <ol>/<nav> so assistive tech reports position in the sequence

multiPageForms.usage: Follows the WAI tutorial for multi-page forms — a labelled step region, a visible progress indicator, and a status message announcing 'Step N of M' on each change
