navigationAPI.usage: Step advance uses Navigation API navigate with history: 'replace' so the Back button exits the flow rather than walking step-by-step; the resumable variant writes the step index to History state (NavigationHistoryEntry.getState)
navigationAPI.adoption: Fallback to History API replaceState for Safari <17.2

invokerCommands.usage: Next/Back/Go-to-step controls use the commandfor/command pattern

declarations.name: StoryCanvasElement
declarations.description: Renders a single WebCase ({ id, title, description, code }) in component isolation — the docs story renderer ratified by #626 Fork 2 (docs examples ARE webcases). The isolated frame keeps the demoed component's styles and scripts from leaking into the docs chrome (and vice-versa), so one fixture serves both the conformance loop and the docs surface. Interaction-tests fold in as webcases carrying an interaction script; docs-only presentation needs (ordering, prose) are optional webcase fields, not a second artifact.

customElements.pattern: Autonomous custom element (<story-canvas>) owning the isolated render lifecycle
customElements.adoption: The element takes a WebCase via property (`case`) or a `case-id` attribute resolved against the page's webcases, and renders code into its isolated frame.

shadowDom.pattern: Shadow root for the story frame (title/description chrome); style-encapsulated from the host page
shadowDom.adoption: The frame chrome lives in shadow DOM; the case render target is isolated separately so the case's own styles don't bleed into the chrome.

iframeIsolation.pattern: Isolated browsing context for the case render target (the strongest style/script boundary)
iframeIsolation.adoption: The default isolation tier mounts the case `code` in a same-origin srcdoc iframe so a case's global CSS/JS can't leak into the docs page; a lighter shadow-only tier is the opt-in for trusted, pure-component cases.

declarations.name: TabsComponent
declarations.description: Accessible tab interface component built on View (ViewBehavior for panel switching), providing tab triggers, panels, and ARIA semantics.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: Shared group name making the panels mutually exclusive — the native exclusive-group (`name`) semantics the ARIA Tabs pattern is built on.

members.name: activeTab
members.description: The id of the currently selected tab; setting it switches the shown panel via the composed View behavior.

slots.name: tab
slots.description: Each tab trigger (uses the `commandfor`/`command` invoker pattern).

slots.name: panel
slots.description: Each tab panel, shown one at a time and wired to its tab via ARIA.

traits.name: withCloseableTabs
traits.description: Adds close buttons to tabs for dismissible panels

traits.name: withReorderableTabs
traits.description: Enables drag-to-reorder for tab triggers

traits.name: withOverflowTabs
traits.description: Handles tab overflow via scrolling or dropdown menu when tabs exceed available width

traits.name: withLazyTabs
traits.description: Renders panel content only on first activation for performance

exclusiveGroups.usage: Panels share name attribute for mutual exclusivity

openUITabs.status: Following Open UI research for future native alignment

declarations.name: Temporal
declarations.description: The abstract core block that realizes the Temporal Intent (date/time selection) — the shared machinery every concrete picker pins down. Native-first by construction: a temporal control IS a native date/time input (<input type="date|time|datetime-local">), so the core's job is only the enhancements native cannot express alone — a spatial/linear presentation surface (calendar grid, clock, scroll wheel), locale-aware parsing and display, and range/multi coordination — all behind one intent contract. It composes the Temporal Intent (presentation × granularity), the Locale Intent (parse/format), the Input Intent (pointer vs keyboard vs touch modality), and Focus Delegation (roving the surface). It ships no concrete picker itself: named shallow presets (date-picker, time-picker, datetime-picker, date-range-picker) pin granularity/presentation and bind their native anchor over this one core.

ariaApgKeyboard.usage: The abstract date/time core: native-first over `<input type="date|time|datetime-local">`, falling back to a `role="dialog"` + `role="grid"` calendar with the APG date-picker keyboard model.

declarations.name: TimePicker
declarations.description: A named shallow preset over the abstract `temporal` core: a single time-of-day picker. It pins the Temporal Intent to presentation=media (a spatial clock) and granularity=point (one time), bound to the native <input type="time"> anchor. Like its date-picker sibling it adds no machinery of its own — clock rendering, locale-aware parse/format, and keyboard roving are the core's; the native time input stays the zero-JS keyboard fallback. (A linear presentation — a scroll wheel — is the common mobile variant; this preset pins media, and a linear member would pin presentation=linear over the same anchor.)

ariaApgKeyboard.usage: A time-of-day preset: native `<input type="time">` or a spatial clock surface.

declarations.name: Toggle/Switch
declarations.description: Native-first over `<input type="checkbox">` with `role="switch"`: a boolean on/off control whose change takes effect IMMEDIATELY, distinct from a checkbox's deferred-until-submit form value. Realizes the `input` boolean control. `aria-checked` true/false reflects on/off; no mixed/indeterminate state (a switch is strictly binary). Zero JS — the native element plus `role="switch"` and styling IS the block. Sliced from #468 (form-control inventory).

attributes.name: checked
attributes.description: On/off state; reflects to `aria-checked` true/false. A switch is strictly binary — no mixed state.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Disables the control; excluded from form submission and the tab order.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: Form field name the on/off value is submitted under (native `<input type="checkbox">`).

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: Value submitted when the switch is on.

members.name: checked
members.description: Gets or sets the on/off state; the change takes effect immediately (distinct from a checkbox’s deferred form value).

ariaApgKeyboard.usage: Native `<input type="checkbox">` with `role="switch"`: a boolean on/off control whose change takes effect immediately; Space/Enter toggle `aria-checked`.

declarations.name: Tooltip
declarations.description: A small, transient, non-interactive surface that describes its trigger on hover or keyboard focus and is wired as the trigger's accessible description (aria-describedby, never aria-label). Native-first: it extends the built-in title attribute and the Popover API's popover="hint" where supported, falling back to an ARIA-described custom bubble only when richer content or collision-aware positioning is needed. It composes the Anchor Intent (placement + flip/shift at the viewport edge), the Surface Intent (floating elevation/material of the bubble), and the Typography Intent (the truncation-recovery case that reads a clipped label). Deliberately distinct from a toggletip (click-revealed, live-region-announced) and from a non-modal popover with interactive content.

attributes.name: for
attributes.description: Id of the trigger this tooltip describes — wired as the trigger’s `aria-describedby` (never `aria-label`).

attributes.name: placement
attributes.description: Anchor placement relative to the trigger, with flip/shift at the viewport edge (composed Anchor Intent).

slots.description: The tooltip content (default slot) — a `role="tooltip"` surface revealed on hover/focus.

ariaApgTooltip.usage: A non-interactive `role="tooltip"` surface revealed on hover or keyboard focus and wired as the trigger’s `aria-describedby`, dismissible with Escape.

declarations.name: TransientElement
declarations.description: Base class and utilities for custom elements that replace themselves with semantically correct native HTML during connectedCallback — enabling context-aware element selection without permanent wrapper nodes.

traits.name: withSelfReplacement
traits.description: Base mixin that handles the microtask-deferred replaceWith() lifecycle, attribute transfer, and child node migration

replaceWith.pattern: Element.replaceWith() replaces an element in its parent's child list with new nodes
replaceWith.adoption: Core mechanism — transient element calls queueMicrotask(() => this.replaceWith(replacement)) from connectedCallback to safely self-replace after callback stack completes

customElements.pattern: Autonomous custom elements with connectedCallback lifecycle
customElements.adoption: Transient elements are autonomous custom elements that run initialization logic in connectedCallback, then replace themselves with native elements

queueMicrotask.pattern: Schedules a microtask to run after current script completes but before rendering
queueMicrotask.adoption: Defers replaceWith() out of connectedCallback stack to avoid re-entrancy with disconnectedCallback

headingElements.pattern: <h1>–<h6> provide document heading hierarchy; WHATWG outline algorithm removed from spec
headingElements.adoption: AutoHeading replaces itself with the correct <hN> element based on nesting depth or injector-provided level

ariaHeadingRole.pattern: role='heading' with aria-level for non-native heading semantics
ariaHeadingRole.adoption: Fallback only — MDN recommends native <hN> elements over role='heading'. Self-replacement produces native elements.

declarations.name: TreeSelectBehavior
declarations.description: The hierarchical member of the droplist family (per the #064 ruling). `TreeSelectBehavior` realizes the `hierarchy` intent: a `role="tree"` of `role="treeitem"` / `role="group"` with `aria-level` / `aria-setsize` / `aria-posinset`, Right/Left expand-then-descend / collapse-then-ascend traversal, Up/Down/Home/End movement, and node selection (`aria-checked`, single|multiple) with an optional `cascade` (a partially-checked parent reads `mixed`). Builds the tree from a declarative `TreeNode[]` and emits `tree-change`. Native-first; degrades conceptually to a grouped `<select>`. First consumer: the auto-insurance coverage builder (#318).

ariaTreeview.usage: A `role="tree"` of `role="treeitem"` nodes with `aria-expanded` on parents and `aria-selected` on the chosen node; arrow keys walk and expand/collapse the hierarchy.

declarations.name: TrustedHTML
declarations.description: Structural directive enforcing Trusted Types policies on dynamic HTML content via comment-based virtual elements.

trustedTypes.usage: A structural directive that enforces a Trusted Types policy on dynamic HTML, routing injected markup through `TrustedHTML` so the sink is XSS-safe.

declarations.name: TrustedHTMLBehavior
declarations.description: Attribute-based behavior that enforces Trusted Types policies on an element's innerHTML mutations.

trustedTypes.usage: An attribute behaviour that enforces a Trusted Types policy on an element’s `innerHTML` mutations via `TrustedHTML`.

declarations.name: TypeAheadBehavior
declarations.description: Keyboard type-ahead navigation for listboxes, menus, selects, and trees: buffer typed characters, move focus to the matching item, then reset after an idle timeout. Moves focus — never filters.

events.name: typeahead-match
events.description: Fired when the buffer matches an item and focus moves to it

events.name: typeahead-nomatch
events.description: Fired when the buffer matches no item (focus is unchanged)

events.name: typeahead-reset
events.description: Fired when the character buffer is cleared after the idle timeout

traits.name: withPrefixMatching
traits.description: Accumulate typed characters into a prefix buffer and match item labels by prefix

traits.name: withSameLetterCycling
traits.description: A repeated single key cycles focus through items starting with that character

traits.name: withWrapAround
traits.description: Wrap focus to the start of the collection when matching passes the last item

traits.name: withTimeoutReset
traits.description: Clear the character buffer after an idle timeout (default 500ms)

ariaApgKeyboard.pattern: Type-ahead / first-character navigation across the Listbox, Menu, Combobox, and Tree patterns
ariaApgKeyboard.adoption: Buffer printable keys, move focus (or aria-activedescendant) to the matching item; reset after idle timeout

htmlSelectTypeAhead.adoption: Mirror the platform default a custom listbox/menu would otherwise lose

keyboardEventKey.adoption: Match single-character, non-modifier keys; ignore Arrow/Enter/Tab and modifier chords

declarations.name: ViewEngine
declarations.description: Foundation for all show/hide/render patterns. Provides a shared engine and specialized blocks (behaviors + directives) that higher-level components build on.

exclusiveAccordion.adoption: ViewEngine uses name attribute for exclusive view groups

hiddenUntilFound.adoption: ViewEngine hidden mode option for searchable hidden content

popoverEvents.pattern: beforetoggle (cancelable) and toggle events with oldState/newState
popoverEvents.adoption: ViewEngine fires same events with same detail shape

contentVisibility.pattern: content-visibility: hidden for performance, auto for lazy rendering
contentVisibility.adoption: ViewEngine default hidden mode (performant, keeps a11y tree)

inertAttribute.pattern: inert makes content non-interactive but visible
inertAttribute.adoption: ViewEngine hidden mode for preview/disabled states

viewTransitionsAPI.adoption: ViewEngine wraps show/hide in view transitions when opted in

domParts.pattern: Low-level API for marking DOM positions for updates
domParts.adoption: Directives use comment markers today, ready to adopt DOM Parts when available

declarations.name: WizardElement
declarations.description: The reference Flow-Progress surface — a `<wizard-flow>` custom element that wires a portable workflow graph through the swappable `customWorkflowEngine` (#634/#650) into the flow-progress UX (register `wizard`). It owns no orchestration: transitions, guards, and completion stay on the engine; the element renders the current position and each step's status. Composes the Stepper block for the linear-presentation mechanics it already owns (`aria-current="step"`, the Step N of M announcement, one-panel-at-a-time switching) and maps the engine's step-transition stream to per-step status (wait/process/finish/error). Back/undo uses the running instance's `back()` (the movement: back-allowed default). Slice A of #651.

ariaCurrentStep.usage: The active step indicator and its panel carry aria-current="step" (delegated to the composed Stepper); the step list is an ordered <ol> conveying position

multiPageForms.usage: A labelled step region, a visible per-step status indicator, and a polite live region announcing 'Step N of M' on each engine transition

declarations.name: WorkflowElement
declarations.description: Multi-step process orchestration without URL changes — wizard-like flows with per-step validation, linear/branching progression, and state persistence. The non-route complement to Router's URL-based step navigation.

events.name: workflow-step-before-change
events.description: Fired before step transition. Cancel to prevent advancement (e.g., failed validation).

events.name: workflow-step-change
events.description: Fired after step transition completes. Step content is now visible.

events.name: workflow-complete
events.description: Fired when user completes the final step. Cancel to prevent submission (e.g., final validation).

events.name: workflow-error
events.description: Fired when step validation fails. Provides the invalid fields and error messages.

traits.name: withLinearProgression
traits.description: Steps must be completed in order; future steps are disabled until current passes validation

traits.name: withNonLinearProgression
traits.description: Steps can be visited in any order; all validated on final submit

traits.name: withBranchingLogic
traits.description: Step sequence adapts dynamically based on user input (progressive wizard)

traits.name: withGateValidation
traits.description: Validates current step fields via Constraint Validation API before allowing advancement

traits.name: withAutoSave
traits.description: Persists step data to sessionStorage/localStorage on each step change

traits.name: withReviewStep
traits.description: Final step displays summary of all collected data for confirmation

constraintValidation.pattern: checkValidity(), reportValidity(), setCustomValidity() for per-step field validation
constraintValidation.adoption: withGateValidation calls checkValidity() on the active step's fieldset before allowing advancement; hidden steps are automatically skipped by the browser

fieldset.pattern: <fieldset> + <legend> for semantic grouping; disabling a fieldset disables all children
fieldset.adoption: Each step is a <fieldset>; inactive steps get disabled attribute to prevent interaction and exclude from form validation

ariaCurrent.pattern: aria-current='step' marks the active step in a numbered indicator
ariaCurrent.adoption: Step indicator (progress rail) uses <ol> with aria-current='step' on the active <li>

invokerCommands.adoption: Next/Previous buttons use commandfor targeting the workflow element with command='--workflow-next' and command='--workflow-prev'

cssScrollSnap.pattern: CSS Scroll Snap for step transitions; scrollsnapchange event for JS hooks
cssScrollSnap.adoption: Optional trait: withSnapTransitions uses scroll-snap-type: x mandatory for horizontal step animation

dialog.pattern: <dialog> for modal wizard flows with native focus trapping
dialog.adoption: When workflow is rendered inside a <dialog>, focus management delegates to the browser's dialog focusing steps

declarations.name: NativeWorkflowEngine
declarations.description: The native-first runtime behind the Workflow Protocol's swappable CustomWorkflowEngine provider seam (#634, webworkflows): a dependency-free interpreter of the portable SCXML-style orchestration graph (steps + transitions + guards + parallel/fork-join + completion) that threads context, tracks the current position, and emits a { flow, from, to, context, at } step-transition event. Honors TIER-1 core + TIER-2 common operators and declares its supported set (the #085 compliance posture). Ships a CustomWorkflowEngineRegistry and a reference XState adapter as an alternative impl.

events.name: step-transition
events.description: Fired on every workflow transition — the composition seam for UX + audit

events.name: workflow-complete
events.description: Fired when the workflow reaches a final step

scxml.pattern: SCXML state-chart transition model (states + transitions + guards + parallel + final)
scxml.adoption: The portable WorkflowGraph is a data-defined SCXML-style interchange; the native engine interprets it directly, and the XState/SCION adapters map onto it.

customEvent.pattern: An observable step-transition event as the composition seam
customEvent.adoption: Every transition emits { flow, from, to, context, at } for the Flow Progress UX and for audit/reporting.
